integrity_note: This file is a frozen canonical benchmark. Any modification after frozen_date requires a version bump and invalidates all prior citations. PATCH for metadata. MINOR for adding pilots. MAJOR for reclassifying or removing pilots.

version_history.change: Full ORI-C pipeline on densified Level C pilots. Pantheon SN -> ACCEPT. PBDB marine -> REJECT. LLM scaling -> REJECT. All 7 now decidable at Level B.

description: Frozen generalization benchmark for the ORI-C framework. Formalizes the 7-pilot corpus as a public, versionable, citable reference object with explicit proof levels, power classes, and known limitations.

canonical_core_reference.note: Level A (canonical) is NOT part of this benchmark. It is the anchor against which generalization is measured.

conclusive_pilots.upgrade_note: Upgraded from C (INDETERMINATE, 100 pts) via densification + full pipeline

rejected_pilots.upgrade_note: Upgraded from C (INDETERMINATE, 100 pts). After densification (140 pts), C1 failed: no transition detected (det_rate=0.0). True negative.
Upgraded from C (INDETERMINATE, 60 pts). After densification (120 pts), C1 failed: no transition detected (sigma_zero_post=1.0). Synthetic-calibrated data.

B.label: Conclusive
B.publishable_as: Confirmed out-of-domain applications (ACCEPT) or confirmed non-detections (REJECT)

known_limitations: Only 7 domains tested; framework incompatibility may emerge in untested domains
No independent external replication yet; all validation by framework authors
min_points_per_segment >= 60 blocks many real-world datasets by design
Borderline power class can still produce conclusive verdicts (BTC, COVID, Pantheon SN prove this)
REJECT verdicts may reflect proxy mapping inadequacy, not necessarily absence of physical threshold
LLM scaling data is synthetic-calibrated, not real benchmark data — REJECT may reflect data quality
citation_note: When citing this benchmark, reference the version and frozen_date. Example: ORI-C Generalization Benchmark v2.0.0 (frozen 2026-03-09), 7 pilots across 7 domains, 5 ACCEPT / 2 REJECT / 0 INDETERMINATE.
